sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.
No calibrated inherited-trait or child-prediction model is supplied with this fixture.
Child phenotype prediction, family inheritance certainty, exact appearance, rare-trait prediction, dermatology outcomes, and medical-risk interpretation are not reported.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived HERC2/OCA2 rs12913832 AG call is available for inherited eye-color context. This does not determine exact eye color and cannot predict a child's eye color.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: OCA2 and HERC2 are important in eye-color genetics, but eye color is influenced by multiple genes and inheritance is not a simple one-marker result.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MC1R rs1805007 CT call is available for inherited hair-color and freckle context. This is not a diagnosis, dermatology result, skin-cancer risk result, or exact appearance prediction.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: MC1R is a well-studied pigmentation gene, but many genes and non-genetic factors contribute to hair color, freckles, and related visible traits.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived EDAR hair-texture/thickness context row is available. This is educational context only and does not predict exact hair texture or a child's trait outcome.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Hair texture and thickness have genetic contributions, but the final trait can reflect multiple genes, development, and non-genetic factors.

unavailableTraits: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.
Child phenotype prediction, family inheritance certainty, exact appearance, rare-trait prediction, dermatology outcomes, and medical-risk sections are unavailable and not inferred.
Eye-color, hair-color/freckle, and hair-texture rows are limited to educational inherited-trait context, not identity, ancestry, diagnosis, or medical-risk interpretation.
plainEnglishLimits: Visible traits are influenced by multiple genes and non-genetic factors.
Direct-to-consumer trait tests provide estimates or context, not certainty about exact appearance.
This report must not be used to identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, guide skincare, or report disease risk.

references.title: MedlinePlus Genetics: Direct-to-consumer genetic test types

missingInputs: Authenticated sample-report sections and rows have not been extracted.
No calibrated inherited-trait probability model or child-prediction model is supplied.
Family phenotype information, parental genotype context, rare-trait evidence, dermatology outcomes, and medical-risk context require separate validated evidence and are not inferred.
Many known and unknown contributors to eye color, hair color, freckles, hair texture, and other inherited visible traits are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Visible traits can be influenced by multiple genes, development, age, environment, and other non-genetic factors.
The significance of individual visible-trait markers may vary across populations and should not be treated as a deterministic appearance or child-phenotype prediction.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, give skincare guidance, diagnose skin conditions, or report melanoma or other skin-cancer risk.
Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are still pending.
